directives.name: state

companions.name: persist-fields
companions.description: No.JS: Comma-separated list of state fields to persist selectively

directives.documentation:
Declares reactive state on the element. The value is evaluated as a JavaScript object literal.

**Example:**
```html
<div state="{ count: 0, name: 'World' }">
```

**Companions:** `persist`, `persist-key`, `persist-fields`

directives.name: store

companions.name: value
companions.description: No.JS: Initial store data as JS object literal

directives.documentation:
Declares a named global store accessible via `$store.name`.

**Example:**
```html
<div store="cart" value="{ items: [], total: 0 }">
```

**Companion:** `value`

directives.name: computed
directives.documentation:
Declares a computed property that auto-updates when dependencies change.

**Example:**
```html
<div computed="fullName" expr="firstName + ' ' + lastName">
```

**Companion:** `expr`

directives.name: watch

companions.name: on:change
companions.description: No.JS: Handler executed on change; receives $old, $new

directives.documentation:
Watches an expression for changes and runs a handler.

**Example:**
```html
<div watch="count" on:change="console.log($old, $new)">
```

**Companion:** `on:change`
**Handler variables:** `$old`, `$new`

directives.name: get

companions.name: as
companions.description: No.JS: Variable name for response data

companions.name: loading
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID shown while loading

companions.name: error
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID shown on error

companions.name: empty
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID shown when response is empty

companions.name: success
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID shown on success

companions.name: redirect
companions.description: No.JS: Path to redirect after success

companions.name: var
companions.description: No.JS: Variable name for additional data

companions.name: into
companions.description: No.JS: Store name to put response into

companions.name: retry-delay
companions.description: No.JS: Delay between retries in ms

companions.name: skeleton
companions.description: No.JS: ID (without #) of a DOM element to hide while loading and restore on response. Prevents CLS — element must start visible in HTML.

directives.documentation:
Performs an HTTP GET request and makes response data available. **Example:** ```html
    • ``` **Companions:** `as`, `loading`, `error`, `empty`, `success`, `then`, `redirect`, `confirm`, `refresh`, `cached`, `body`, `headers`, `var`, `into`, `retry`, `retry-delay`, `params`, `debounce`, `skeleton`

directives.name: post
directives.documentation:
Performs an HTTP POST request. Same companions as `get`.

**Example:**
```html
<form post="/api/users" body="{ name, email }" as="result">
```

directives.name: put
directives.documentation: Performs an HTTP PUT request. Same companions as `get`.

directives.name: patch
directives.documentation: Performs an HTTP PATCH request. Same companions as `get`.

directives.name: delete
directives.documentation: Performs an HTTP DELETE request. Same companions as `get`.

directives.name: bind
directives.documentation:
Binds element's text content to a JS expression. Updates reactively. **Example:** ```html

```

directives.name: bind-html
directives.documentation:
Binds element's innerHTML to a JS expression.

**Example:**
```html
<div bind-html="richContent">
```

directives.name: model
directives.documentation:
Two-way data binding for form elements (INPUT, SELECT, TEXTAREA).

**Example:**
```html
<input model="username">
<select model="selectedOption">
<textarea model="message">
```

directives.name: if

companions.name: then
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID to render when true

companions.name: else
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID to render when false

directives.documentation:
Conditionally renders the element. Removes from DOM when expression is false. **Example:** ```html
Welcome!
Hello Guest
Please log in
``` **Companions:** `then`, `else`, `animate-enter`, `animate-leave`, `transition`, `animate-duration`

directives.name: else-if

companions.name: then
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID to render when true

directives.documentation:
Conditional branch after `if`. Must follow a sibling with `if` or `else-if`. **Example:** ```html
Admin panel
``` **Companion:** `then`

directives.name: else
directives.documentation:
Fallback branch after `if` or `else-if`. Must follow a sibling with `if` or `else-if`. **Example:** ```html
Default content
``` **Companion:** `then`

directives.name: show
directives.documentation:
Toggles element visibility via CSS `display`. Element stays in DOM. **Example:** ```html
Visible when true
``` **Companions:** `animate-enter`, `animate-leave`, `transition`, `animate-duration`

directives.name: hide
directives.documentation:
Opposite of `show`. Hides element via CSS `display: none` when true. **Example:** ```html
Content
```

directives.name: switch
directives.documentation:
Switch statement. Children use `case`, `default`, and `then` attributes. **Example:** ```html

Admin panel

Dashboard

Guest view

```

directives.name: case
directives.valueDescription: Value to match against parent switch expression
directives.documentation:
Switch case. Used inside a `switch` container. **Example:** ```html

Admin panel

```

directives.name: default
directives.documentation:
Default case in a `switch` block. Renders when no `case` matches. **Example:** ```html

Guest view

```

directives.name: each

companions.name: offset
companions.description: No.JS: Number of items to skip

companions.name: template
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID for item rendering

companions.name: else
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID for empty list

companions.name: key
companions.description: No.JS: Unique key expression for DOM optimization

companions.name: animate-stagger
companions.description: No.JS: Stagger delay between items in ms

directives.documentation:
Alias of `foreach`. Loops over an iterable collection using `"item in list"` syntax. Supports inline children as template.

**Example:**
```html
<li each="user in users" bind="user.name">
<li each="item in products" filter="item.price > 10" sort="name" limit="5">
```

**Loop context variables:** `$index`, `$count`, `$first`, `$last`, `$even`, `$odd`

**Companions:** `index`, `filter`, `sort`, `limit`, `offset`, `template`, `else`, `key`, `animate-enter`, `animate-leave`, `animate-stagger`, `animate-duration`

directives.name: foreach

companions.name: offset
companions.description: No.JS: Number of items to skip

companions.name: template
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID for item rendering

companions.name: else
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID for empty list

companions.name: key
companions.description: No.JS: Unique key expression for DOM optimization

companions.name: animate-stagger
companions.description: No.JS: Stagger delay between items in ms

directives.documentation:
Primary iteration directive. Loops over an iterable using `"item in list"` syntax. Supports both **inline children** (element's own content as template) and **external templates** (via `template` companion). Aliases: `each`, `for`.

**Inline template (recommended):**
```html
<li foreach="user in users" bind="user.name">
<li foreach="item in products" filter="item.price > 10" sort="name" limit="5">
```

**External template:**
```html
<li foreach="item in products" template="item-tpl">
```

**Loop context variables:** `$index`, `$count`, `$first`, `$last`, `$even`, `$odd`

**Companions:** `index`, `filter`, `sort`, `limit`, `offset`, `template`, `else`, `key`, animation attrs

directives.name: for

companions.name: offset
companions.description: No.JS: Number of items to skip

companions.name: template
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID for item rendering

companions.name: else
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID for empty list

companions.name: key
companions.description: No.JS: Unique key expression for DOM optimization

companions.name: animate-stagger
companions.description: No.JS: Stagger delay between items in ms

directives.documentation:
Alias of `foreach`. Loops over an iterable collection using `"item in list"` syntax. Supports inline children as template.

**Example:**
```html
<li for="user in users" bind="user.name">
<li for="item in products" filter="item.price > 10" sort="name" limit="5">
```

**Loop context variables:** `$index`, `$count`, `$first`, `$last`, `$even`, `$odd`

**Companions:** `index`, `filter`, `sort`, `limit`, `offset`, `template`, `else`, `key`, `animate-enter`, `animate-leave`, `animate-stagger`, `animate-duration`

directives.name: trigger
directives.documentation:
Dispatches a custom event.

**Example:**
```html
<button on:click="count++" trigger="countChanged" trigger-data="count">
```

**Companion:** `trigger-data`

directives.name: ref
directives.documentation:
Declares a reference to this element, accessible via `$refs.name`.

**Example:**
```html
<input ref="emailInput">
<!-- Access: $refs.emailInput.value -->
```

directives.name: use
directives.documentation: Inserts a `

directives.name: call

companions.name: as
companions.description: No.JS: Variable name for response data (default: "data")

companions.name: loading
companions.description: No.JS: Template ID shown while loading

companions.name: redirect
companions.description: No.JS: Path to redirect after success

directives.documentation:
HTTP call triggered by user action.

**Example:**
```html
<button call="/api/delete/1" method="delete" confirm="Are you sure?" loading="spinner-tpl">
```

**Companions:** `method`, `as`, `loading`, `into`, `success`, `error`, `then`, `redirect`, `confirm`, `body`, `headers`

directives.name: validate

companions.name: error
companions.description: No.JS: Error message or template ID (prefix with #)

companions.name: error-*
companions.description: No.JS: Per-rule error message or template ID (e.g. error-required, error-email)

companions.name: error-class
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class(es) applied to invalid fields

companions.name: validate-on
companions.description: No.JS: Validation trigger events (space-separated: input, blur, focusout, submit)

companions.name: validate-if
companions.description: No.JS: Conditional expression; field is only validated when truthy

companions.name: as
companions.description: No.JS: Expose per-field validation state as a context variable

directives.documentation:
Form validation with pristine-aware errors. **On a form:** creates `$form` context with `valid`, `dirty`, `touched`, `pending`, `errors`, `values`, `fields`, `firstError`, `errorCount`, `reset()`. **On a field:** standalone validation with error template. **Example:** ```html
``` **Built-in validators:** `required`, `email`, `url`, `min`, `max`, `custom` **Companions:** `error`, `error-*`, `error-class`, `validate-on`, `validate-if`, `as`, `success`

directives.name: error-boundary
directives.documentation:
Wraps content with error handling. Shows fallback template on error. **Example:** ```html
```

directives.name: t

companions.name: t-html
companions.description: No.JS: Render translation as sanitized HTML (via _sanitizeHtml()) instead of plain text

directives.documentation:
i18n translation. Value is the translation key.

**Example:**
```html
<span t="greeting" t-name="World">
```

**Companions:** `t-*` (interpolation parameters), `t-html`

Interpolation params are passed as `t-{param}` attributes.

directives.name: i18n-ns
directives.documentation:
Sets i18n namespace for child elements. When used without a value on `route-view`, the namespace is auto-detected from the template name. **Example:** ```html
```

directives.name: drag

companions.name: drag-type
companions.description: No.JS: Type identifier for the dragged item

companions.name: drag-effect
companions.description: No.JS: Drag effect (copy, move, link, none)

companions.name: drag-handle
companions.description: No.JS: CSS selector for drag handle

companions.name: drag-image
companions.description: No.JS: CSS selector for custom drag image

companions.name: drag-ghost-class
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class for ghost element

directives.documentation:
Makes element draggable.

**Example:**
```html
<div drag="item" drag-type="card" drag-class="dragging">
```

**Companions:** `drag-type`, `drag-effect`, `drag-handle`, `drag-image`, `drag-image-offset`, `drag-disabled`, `drag-class`, `drag-ghost-class`, `drag-group`

directives.name: drop

companions.name: drop-effect
companions.description: No.JS: Drop effect (copy, move, link, none)

companions.name: drop-class
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class on valid dragover

companions.name: drop-reject-class
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class on invalid dragover

directives.documentation:
Makes element a drop target.

**Example:**
```html
<div drop="items.push($drag)" drop-accept="card" drop-class="over">
```

**Handler variables:** `$drag`, `$dragType`, `$dragEffect`, `$dropIndex`, `$source`, `$target`, `$el`

directives.name: drag-list

companions.name: drag-list-item
companions.description: No.JS: Item variable name in template

companions.name: drag-list-remove
companions.description: No.JS: Remove from source after drag

companions.name: drop-class
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class on valid dragover

companions.name: drop-reject-class
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class on invalid dragover

companions.name: drop-settle-class
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class during settle animation

companions.name: drop-empty-class
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class when list is empty

directives.documentation:
Sortable list with drag and drop support.

**Example:**
```html
<ul drag-list="items" template="item-tpl" drag-type="card">
```

directives.name: drag-multiple

companions.name: drag-multiple-class
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class for selected items

directives.documentation:
Enables multi-select drag on `drag` elements.

**Example:**
```html
<div drag="item" drag-multiple drag-group="cards">
```

**Companions:** `drag-group` (required), `drag-multiple-class`

directives.name: base
directives.documentation:
Sets the base URL for all descendant fetch directives (`get`, `post`, `put`, `patch`, `delete`, `call`). **Example:** ```html
```

directives.name: route
directives.valueDescription: Route path pattern or "*" for catch-all wildcard

companions.name: route-active
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class added when route is active (default: 'active')

companions.name: route-active-exact
companions.description: No.JS: CSS class added only on exact path match

companions.name: lazy
companions.description: No.JS: Loading strategy for route template (priority or ondemand)

companions.name: page-title
companions.description: No.JS: Sets document.title on each navigation. Value is a JS expression; use single-quoted strings: page-title="'About | Site'". Can access $route and $store.

companions.name: page-description
companions.description: No.JS: Creates/updates <meta name="description"> on each navigation. Value is a JS expression.

companions.name: page-canonical
companions.description: No.JS: Creates/updates <link rel="canonical"> on each navigation. Value is a JS expression.

companions.name: page-jsonld
companions.description: No.JS: Creates/updates <script type="application/ld+json" data-nojs> on each navigation. Value is a JSON string with {placeholder} interpolation.

directives.documentation: Turns an `` element into a route link that navigates without page reload. Also used on `

directives.name: route-view
directives.valueDescription: Outlet name (optional, empty for default outlet)

companions.name: i18n-ns
companions.description: No.JS: i18n namespace for route templates

companions.name: transition
companions.description: No.JS: View Transition API preset or custom name for route changes. Built-in presets: slide, fade, scale, none.

directives.documentation:
Route rendering outlet. Displays the template matching the current route. Uses the **View Transition API** by default for route transitions (configurable via `router.viewTransition`). The `transition` attribute accepts built-in presets or a custom name. **Built-in presets:** - `slide` — horizontal slide between old and new content - `fade` — crossfade between old and new content - `scale` — scale down old content, scale up new content - `none` — disable transition animation CSS uses `::view-transition-old(route-content)` / `::view-transition-new(route-content)` pseudo-elements. **Example:** ```html
``` **Config:** `router.viewTransition` (default: `true`) **Companions:** `src`, `ext`, `route-index`, `i18n-ns`, `transition`

directives.name: guard

companions.name: redirect
companions.description: No.JS: Path to redirect to when guard fails

directives.documentation: Route guard that prevents navigation when the expression is falsy. **Example:** ```html ``` **Companion:** `redirect`

directives.name: include
directives.documentation: Synchronously clones an inline template into the current position. Used on `

directives.name: page-title
directives.valueDescription: JS expression evaluating to a string
directives.documentation:
Updates `document.title` reactively. Place on a `
` element for non-routing pages. For SPA routes prefer the `page-title` attribute on `

directives.name: page-description
directives.valueDescription: JS expression evaluating to a string
directives.documentation:
Creates or updates `` in ``. Place on a `
` element. Reactive — re-applies on state changes. **Example:** ```html
```

directives.name: page-canonical
directives.valueDescription: JS expression evaluating to a URL string
directives.documentation:
Creates or updates `` in ``. Place on a `
` element. Reactive — re-applies on state changes. **Example:** ```html
```

directives.name: page-jsonld
directives.valueDescription: JSON-LD template in element body with {placeholder} expressions
directives.documentation: Creates or updates `

patterns.name: bind-*
patterns.documentation: Dynamically binds an HTML attribute to a JS expression. **Example:** ```html Link ``` **Boolean attributes:** `disabled`, `readonly`, `checked`, `selected`, `hidden`, `required` **Common targets:** `href`, `src`, `class`, `title`, `alt`, `placeholder`, `action`, `value`

patterns.name: class-*
patterns.valueDescription: JS boolean expression (or object/array for map/list)
patterns.documentation:
Conditionally applies CSS class(es).

**Example:**
```html
<div class-active="isActive">
<div class-map="{ active: isActive, hidden: !show }">
<div class-list="['base', isActive ? 'active' : '']">
```

**Sub-behaviors:** `class-map` (object → classes), `class-list` (array → classes)

patterns.name: style-*
patterns.documentation:
Dynamically sets a CSS style property. Attribute name is kebab-case, converted to camelCase.

**Example:**
```html
<div style-color="textColor">
<div style-font-size="fontSize + 'px'">
<div style-map="{ color: textColor, fontSize: size + 'px' }">
```

**Sub-behavior:** `style-map` (object → styles)

patterns.name: on:*
patterns.documentation:
Listens for DOM events and executes JS statement(s).

**Example:**
```html
<button on:click="count++">Increment</button>
<form on:submit.prevent="handleSubmit()">
<input on:keydown.enter="search()">
```

**Modifiers:** `.prevent`, `.stop`, `.once`, `.self`, `.debounce`, `.throttle`
**Key modifiers:** `.enter`, `.escape`, `.tab`, `.space`, `.delete`, `.backspace`, `.up`, `.down`, `.left`, `.right`, `.ctrl`, `.alt`, `.shift`, `.meta`

**Handler variables:** `$event`, `$el`
